entries.definition:
ḥ–b–b grains, seeds, plants, bulbs; core of the heart, affection, love, to love, to prefer; loved one, a friend; dew. Of this root, eight forms occur 95 times in the Qurʾan: حَبَّبَ ḥabbaba once; أَحَبَّ ʾaḥabba 64 times; ٱسْتَحَبَّ istaḥabba four times; حُبّ ḥubb nine times; أَحَبّ ʾaḥabb three times; أَحِبَّآء aḥibbāʾ once; مَحَبَّة maḥabbatun once; حَبّ ḥabb seven times and حَبَّة ḥabbatun five times.
حَبَّبَ ḥabbaba [v. II, trans.] to render likeable, to cause something to be loved, liked or accepted, to endear something (49:7) وَلَٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ حَبَّبَ إِلَيْكُمُ ٱلْإِيمَٰنَ وَزَيَّنَهُۥ فِى قُلُوبِكُمْ but God has endeared faith to you and made it beautiful in your hearts.

أَحَبَّ ʾaḥabba [v. IV, trans.] 1 to like (49:12) أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَن يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتًا would any of you like to eat the flesh of his brother [even] dead? 2 to want dearly, to wish, to desire (9:108) فِيهِ رِجَالٌ يُحِبُّونَ أَن يَتَطَهَّرُوا۟ in it are men who desire to grow in purity.

ٱسْتَحَبَّ ʾastaḥabba [v. X, trans.] to prefer, to find more likeable, desirable (41:17) فَٱسْتَحَبُّوا۟ ٱلْعَمَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ as for Thamūd, We showed them the right way, but they preferred blindness to guidance.

حُبّ ḥubb [n.] love (2:165) وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَتَّخِذُ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَندَادًا يُحِبُّونَهُمْ كَحُبِّ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَشَدُّ حُبًّا لِّلَّهِ there are some who choose to worship others besides God as rivals to Him, loving them with the love due to God, but the believers have greater love for God; *(12:30) قَدْ شَغَفَهَا حُبًّا he has smitten her with love [lit. penetrated the core of

187
entries.definition_english:
ḥ–b–b grains, seeds, plants, bulbs; core of the heart, affection, love, to love, to prefer; loved one, a friend; dew. Of this root, eight forms occur 95 times in the Qurʾan: حَبَّبَ ḥabbaba once; أَحَبَّ ʾaḥabba 64 times; ٱسْتَحَبَّ istaḥabba four times; حُبّ ḥubb nine times; أَحَبّ ʾaḥabb three times; أَحِبَّآء aḥibbāʾ once; مَحَبَّة maḥabbatun once; حَبّ ḥabb seven times and حَبَّة ḥabbatun five times.
حَبَّبَ ḥabbaba [v. II, trans.] to render likeable, to cause something to be loved, liked or accepted, to endear something (49:7) وَلَٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ حَبَّبَ إِلَيْكُمُ ٱلْإِيمَٰنَ وَزَيَّنَهُۥ فِى قُلُوبِكُمْ but God has endeared faith to you and made it beautiful in your hearts.

أَحَبَّ ʾaḥabba [v. IV, trans.] 1 to like (49:12) أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَن يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتًا would any of you like to eat the flesh of his brother [even] dead? 2 to want dearly, to wish, to desire (9:108) فِيهِ رِجَالٌ يُحِبُّونَ أَن يَتَطَهَّرُوا۟ in it are men who desire to grow in purity.

ٱسْتَحَبَّ ʾastaḥabba [v. X, trans.] to prefer, to find more likeable, desirable (41:17) فَٱسْتَحَبُّوا۟ ٱلْعَمَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ as for Thamūd, We showed them the right way, but they preferred blindness to guidance.

حُبّ ḥubb [n.] love (2:165) وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَتَّخِذُ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَندَادًا يُحِبُّونَهُمْ كَحُبِّ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَشَدُّ حُبًّا لِّلَّهِ there are some who choose to worship others besides God as rivals to Him, loving them with the love due to God, but the believers have greater love for God; *(12:30) قَدْ شَغَفَهَا حُبًّا he has smitten her with love [lit. penetrated the core of

187

entries.definition:
her heart]; *(76:8) وَيُطْعِمُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِۦ and they give food, for the love of Him [also interpreted as: even though they long for it themselves].
أَحَبّ ʾaḥabb [elat.] 1 dearer, more loved (12:8) إِذْ قَالُوا۟ لَيُوسُفُ وَأَخُوهُ أَحَبُّ إِلَىٰٓ أَبِينَا مِنَّا when they said [to each other], ‘Joseph and his brother are indeed dearer to our father than we are’ 2 more desirable, dearer, preferable (12:33) قَالَ رَبِّ ٱلسِّجْنُ أَحَبُّ إِلَىَّ مِمَّا يَدْعُونَنِىٓ إِلَيْهِ he said, ‘My Lord!, the prison is more desirable to me than what they are asking me to do.’

أَحِبَّآء aḥibbāʾ [pl. of n. حَبِيب ḥabīb] loved ones (5:18) وَقَالَتِ ٱلْيَهُودُ وَٱلنَّصَٰرَىٰ نَحْنُ أَبْنَٰٓؤُا۟ ٱللَّهِ وَأَحِبَّٰٓؤُهُۥ the Jews and the Christians say, ‘We are the children of God and His beloved ones.’

مَحَبَّة maḥabbatun [v. n./n.] love (20:39) وَأَلْقَيْتُ عَلَيْكَ مَحَبَّةً مِّنِّى and I showered My love [lit. love from Me] upon you.

حَبّ ḥabb [coll. n.] 1 grains (50:9) وَنَزَّلْنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً مُّبَٰرَكًا فَأَنۢبَتْنَا بِهِۦ جَنَّٰتٍ وَحَبَّ ٱلْحَصِيدِ and We send down from the sky blessed water, and with it grow gardens and grain of harvested crops 2 seed (6:95) إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ فَالِقُ ٱلْحَبِّ وَٱلنَّوَىٰ it is God who splits open the seed and the fruit stone: He brings out the living from the dead.

حَبَّة ḥabbatun [n.] a single grain (2:261) مَّثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَٰلَهُمْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ كَمَثَلِ حَبَّةٍ أَنۢبَتَتْ سَبْعَ سَنَابِلَ فِى كُلِّ سُنبُلَةٍ مِّا۟ئَةُ حَبَّةٍ the likeness of those who spend their wealth in God’s cause is as the likeness of a grain that produces seven ears, each bearing a hundred grains.
entries.definition_english:
her heart]; *(76:8) وَيُطْعِمُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِۦ and they give food, for the love of Him [also interpreted as: even though they long for it themselves].
